title: Server-Side Functions
author: Gallagher, James
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: This slide deck was presented at the Software Developer’s Workshop was held in Melbourne, Australia from October 17-18, 2007 for the Australia Bureau of Meteorology (BOM). The workshop targeted developers and focused on building custom modules, adding support for new data formats and new service protocols to, as well as extending the server to support server-side functions and other protocols. Developers learned how to enable client applications to access OPeNDAP data servers using several different client libraries. Summary of the slide deck: The slide deck focuses on Server-Side Functions in the context of DAP2 (Data Access Protocol Version 2) and Constraint Expressions (CEs). It starts with an overview of DAP2 interface and CE syntax, demonstrating how URLs are structured to retrieve specific data subsets from datasets. Various examples illustrate CE projection, selection, and functions, showcasing how to choose fields, apply selections, and invoke functions like grid() and geogrid() to manipulate data. The presentation also delves into writing CE functions using C++ and integrating them into the Constraint Evaluator class, detailing steps for writing, compiling, and testing custom functions. Additionally, it discusses the evaluation process of CEs, data retrieval mechanisms, and the role of libraries like libdap. The presentation concludes with a summary of the key points and a demonstration of a simple "Hello World" function added to the FreeForm handler.
